briefs.name: Parlin Power Plant
briefs.notes: Status discrepancy: Global Energy Monitor shows as operating (last updated Jan 2026) while provided data indicates retired. Plant has excellent data center characteristics - 135MW capacity, natural gas infrastructure, direct 500kV transmission access via Neptune Cable system to Long Island, strategic location in Sayreville industrial corridor. Site near major transmission infrastructure including converter station for undersea cable to Long Island.
briefs.acquirability_notes: Major utility owner with established infrastructure, but recent Global Energy Monitor data shows as operating, creating uncertainty about actual retirement status
briefs.partnership_path: Direct approach to Con Edison corporate development team or explore joint venture opportunities through their energy transition initiatives. Con Edison has divested other power plants and may be open to site partnerships for data center development given their grid modernization focus.

brief.opportunity_summary: Parlin Power Plant is a 135MW retired natural gas plant in Sayreville, Middlesex County, NJ with transmission infrastructure (230kV substation 0.06 mi away). Former NRG gas-fired plant in Middlesex County industrial corridor. Adjacent to Raritan River. Sayreville has strong industrial zoning.

acquirability.owner_motivation: Asset disposition — NRG Energy retiring/divesting older generation assets
acquirability.recommended_approach: Direct outreach to NRG Energy asset disposition. NRG has been divesting older assets.
acquirability.notes: Major utility owner with established infrastructure, but recent Global Energy Monitor data shows as operating, creating uncertainty about actual retirement status

developer_value.comparable_transactions: NJ data center land trades at $300K-$1M/MW of supportable capacity in Tier 1 markets

next_steps: Verify current ownership via Middlesex County property records
Contact NRG Energy about site disposition/partnership
Confirm utility interconnection availability with PSEG commercial team
Request Phase I ESA or review existing environmental reports
Drive the site with Ralph — assess physical condition, access, surrounding uses
brief.bpu_contact_relevant: Yes — PSEG territory. Brian's BPU contacts can validate power availability and interconnection timeline.

briefs.name: Mickleton Station
briefs.notes: 71.2 MW retired natural gas combustion turbine. Constellation completed $16.4B Calpine acquisition Jan 2026, creating largest US power producer focused on AI/data center market. Site generated only 68.2 MWh in Q4 2023 before retirement, indicating minimal recent operation. Located in Gloucester County with existing transmission infrastructure.
briefs.acquirability_notes: Constellation Energy (following Calpine acquisition in Jan 2026) is actively focusing on data center partnerships and divesting non-core assets. Small retired peaker plants may be available for redevelopment.
briefs.partnership_path: Approach Constellation Energy directly regarding retired asset disposition. Company is actively monetizing power infrastructure for data center market and may consider site partnerships or sales for smaller non-core assets.

brief.opportunity_summary: Mickleton Station is a 71MW retired natural gas plant in East Greenwich, Gloucester County, NJ with transmission infrastructure (230kV substation 0.01 mi away). South Jersey location near NJ Turnpike. Good highway access. Lower land costs than North Jersey.

acquirability.owner_motivation: Asset disposition — PSEG Power retiring/divesting older generation assets
acquirability.recommended_approach: PSEG partnership. South Jersey sites offer cost advantage. Fiber build required.
acquirability.notes: Constellation Energy (following Calpine acquisition in Jan 2026) is actively focusing on data center partnerships and divesting non-core assets. Small retired peaker plants may be available for redevelopment.

developer_value.comparable_transactions: NJ data center land trades at $300K-$1M/MW of supportable capacity in Tier 1 markets

briefs.name: Chambers Cogeneration LP
briefs.notes: 285MW retired coal plant in Salem County NJ that closed May 2022. Unlike sister plant Logan which was demolished, Chambers structures remain intact. Strong transmission infrastructure, adjacent to Chemours chemical complex, confirmed battery storage redevelopment plans indicate viable grid connection and regulatory approval pathway.
briefs.acquirability_notes: Joint ownership between Starwood Energy (60%) and Atlantic Power (40%) with committed battery storage redevelopment plans but no active demolition yet
briefs.current_owner: Starwood Energy Group Global LLC (60%), Atlantic Power Corporation (40%)
briefs.redevelopment_plans: Battery energy storage facility development as part of $1 billion joint venture with Energy Management Inc to store renewable energy
briefs.partnership_path: Approach Starwood Energy Group directly as they are leading the battery storage redevelopment, or potentially work with both Starwood and Atlantic Power given joint ownership structure

brief.opportunity_summary: Chambers Cogeneration LP is a 285MW retired coal/biomass plant in Carneys Point, Salem County, NJ with transmission infrastructure (230kV substation 0.08 mi away). Large cogeneration site in Salem County. 285MW coal/biomass. Environmental remediation likely needed.

acquirability.owner_motivation: Asset disposition — Atlantic Power Corporation retiring/divesting older generation assets
acquirability.recommended_approach: Atlantic Power has been divesting. Acquisition feasible but remediation costs factor in.
acquirability.notes: Joint ownership between Starwood Energy (60%) and Atlantic Power (40%) with committed battery storage redevelopment plans but no active demolition yet
